key_insight: An impedance-guided optimization framework lets modular soft metamaterials transmit localized deformation in programmable, end-to-end ways across assemblies.
engineering_relevance: This enables custom soft robotic, biomedical, and flexible-electronics systems that can route motion around obstacles, tolerate defects, and support embodied sensing/computation.
method: Build a nonlinear assembly model with position-dependent interactions.
Embed mechanical impedance into unit-cell topology optimization.
Synthesize homogeneous and heterogeneous module families for reassemblable architectures.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports physical realization of multiple on-demand demonstrations, including obstacle-bypassing assemblies, defect-tolerant gripping, and mechanically driven LED and finger-motion sensing devices.
watch_item: Performance likely depends on accurate nonlinear modeling and precise module fabrication/assembly, which may limit robustness outside the designed configurations.
paper_title: Impedance-Guided Programmable Transmission of Localized Deformation in Modular Soft Metamaterials
